AlgebraicKernel_d_1.name: AlgebraicKernel_d_1
AlgebraicKernel_d_1.brief: A model of the AlgebraicKernel_d_1 concept is meant to provide the algebraic functionalities on univariate polynomials of general degree $ d$ .
AlgebraicKernel_d_1.detailed: Refines CopyConstructible Assignable Has models CGAL::Algebraic_kernel_d_1 AlgebraicKernel_d_2 A model of AlgebraicKernel_d_1 must provide:

members.name: Coefficient
members.brief: A model of IntegralDomain and RealEmbeddable .
members.detailed: ExplicitInteroperable with AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Bound .

members.name: Polynomial_1
members.brief: A univariate polynomial that is a model of Polynomial_d , where CGAL::Polynomial_traits_d<Polynomial_1>::Innermost_coefficient_type is AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Coefficient .

members.name: Algebraic_real_1
members.brief: A type that is used to represent real roots of univariate polynomials.
members.detailed: The type must be a model of DefaultConstructible , CopyConstructible , Assignable and RealEmbeddable .

members.name: Bound
members.brief: A type to represent upper and lower bounds of AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Algebraic_real_1 .
members.detailed: The type is ExplicitInteroperable with AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Coefficient and must be a model IntegralDomain , RealEmbeddable and dense in $ \mathbb{R}$ .

members.brief: Size type (unsigned integral type).

members.name: Multiplicity_type
members.brief: Multiplicity type (unsigned integral type).

AlgebraicKernel_d_1::ApproximateAbsolute.name: AlgebraicKernel_d_1::ApproximateAbsolute_1
AlgebraicKernel_d_1::ApproximateAbsolute.brief: A model of AlgebraicKernel_d_1::ApproximateAbsolute_1 is an AdaptableBinaryFunction that computes an approximation of an AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Algebraic_real_1 value with respect to a given absolute precision.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: The function computes a pair $ p$ of AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Bound , where $ p.first$ represents the lower approximation and $ p.second$ represents the upper approximation.
members.detailed: The pair $ p$ approximates the given value $ x$ with respect to the given absolute precision $ a$ . $ p.first <= x $ $ x <= p.second $ $ (x - p.first) <= 2^{-a} $ $ (p.second - x) <= 2^{-a} $

AlgebraicKernel_d_1::ApproximateRelative.name: AlgebraicKernel_d_1::ApproximateRelative_1
AlgebraicKernel_d_1::ApproximateRelative.brief: A model of AlgebraicKernel_d_1::ApproximateRelative_1 is an AdaptableBinaryFunction that computes an approximation of an AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Algebraic_real_1 value with respect to a given relative precision.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: The function computes a pair $ p$ of AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Bound , where $ p.first$ represents the lower approximation and $ p.second$ represents the upper approximation.
members.detailed: The pair $ p$ approximates the given value $ x$ with respect to the given relative precision $ r$ . $ p.first <= x $ $ x <= p.second $ $ (x - p.first) <= 2^{-r} *|x|$ $ (p.second - x) <= 2^{-r} *|x|$

AlgebraicKernel_d_1::ComputePolynomial_1.name: AlgebraicKernel_d_1::ComputePolynomial_1
AlgebraicKernel_d_1::ComputePolynomial_1.brief: Computes a square free univariate polynomial $ p$ , such that the given AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Algebraic_real_1 is a root of $ p$ .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Computes a square free polynomial $ p$ , such that $ x$ is a real root of $ p$ .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: introduces an AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Algebraic_real_1 initialized to $ a$ .

members.name: operator()

members.name: operator()
members.brief: introduces an AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Algebraic_real_1 initialized to $ a$ .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: introduces an AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Algebraic_real_1 initialized to the $ i$ -th real root of $ p$ .
members.detailed: The index starts at $ 0$ , that is, $ p$ must have at least $ i+1$ real roots. $ p$ is square free. $ p$ has at least $ i+1$ real roots.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: introduces an AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Algebraic_real_1 initialized to the only real root of $ p$ in the open interval $ I = (l,u)$ .
members.detailed: $ l < u$ $ p$ is square free. $ p$ has exactly one real root in $ I$ $ p$ has no real root on $ \partial I$

AlgebraicKernel_d_1::IsCoprime_1.name: AlgebraicKernel_d_1::IsCoprime_1
AlgebraicKernel_d_1::IsCoprime_1.brief: Determines whether a given pair of univariate polynomials $ p_1, p_2$ is coprime, namely if $ \deg({\rm gcd}(p_1 ,p_2)) = 0$ .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Returns true if p1 and p2 are coprime.

AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Isolate_1.name: AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Isolate_1
AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Isolate_1.brief: Computes an open isolating interval for an AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Algebraic_real_1 with respect to the real roots of a given univariate polynomial.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Computes an open isolating interval $ I=(l,u)$ for $ a$ with respect to the real roots of $ p$ .
members.detailed: It is not required that $ a$ is a root of $ p$ . $ a \in I$ . $ p(x) \neq0 | \forall x \in\overline{I}\backslash a$ .

AlgebraicKernel_d_1::IsSquareFree_1.name: AlgebraicKernel_d_1::IsSquareFree_1
AlgebraicKernel_d_1::IsSquareFree_1.brief: Computes whether the given univariate polynomial is square free.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Returns true if $ p$ is square free.

AlgebraicKernel_d_1::IsZeroAt_1.name: AlgebraicKernel_d_1::IsZeroAt_1
AlgebraicKernel_d_1::IsZeroAt_1.brief: Computes whether an AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Polynomial_1 is zero at a given AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Algebraic_real_1 .

members.name: result_type
members.brief: Type convertible to bool .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Computes whether $ p$ is zero at $ x$ .

AlgebraicKernel_d_1::MakeCoprime_1.name: AlgebraicKernel_d_1::MakeCoprime_1
AlgebraicKernel_d_1::MakeCoprime_1.brief: Computes for a given pair of univariate polynomials $ p_1$ , $ p_2$ their common part $ g$ up to a constant factor and coprime parts $ q_1$ , $ q_2$ respectively.
AlgebraicKernel_d_1::MakeCoprime_1.detailed: That is, it computes $ g, q_1, q_2$ such that: $ c_1 \cdot p_1 = g \cdot q_1$ for some constant $ c_1$ and $ c_2 \cdot p_2 = g \cdot q_2$ for some constant $ c_2$ , such that $ q_1$ and $ q_2$ are coprime. It returns true if $ p_1$ and $ p_2$ are already coprime. Refines AdaptableQuinaryFunction AlgebraicKernel_d_1::IsCoprime_1

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Computes $ g, q_1, q_2$ as described above.
members.detailed: Returns whether $ p_1$ and $ p_2$ where already coprime.
members.argsstring: (const AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Polynomial_1 &p1, const AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Polynomial_1 &p2, AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Polynomial_1 &g, AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Polynomial_1 &q1, AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Polynomial_1 &q2)

AlgebraicKernel_d_1::MakeSquareFree_1.brief: Returns a square free part of a univariate polynomial.

members.brief: Returns a square free part of $ p$ .

AlgebraicKernel_d_1::SignAt_1.brief: Computes the sign of a univariate polynomial AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Polynomial_1 at a real value of type AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Algebraic_real_1 .

AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Solve_1.name: AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Solve_1
AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Solve_1.brief: Computes the real roots of a univariate polynomial.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Computes all real solutions of $ p$ with multiplicity, and copies them as objects of type std::pair< AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Algebraic_real_1 , AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Multiplicity_type > in res .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Computes all real solutions of $ p$ , and copies them as objects of type AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Algebraic_real_1 in res .
members.detailed: The bool known_to_be_square_free indicates whether $ p$ is known to be square free. Each root, though it might be a multiple root, is reported only once.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Computes all real solutions of $ p$ in the closed interval $ [l,u]$ with multiplicity, and copies them as objects of type std::pair< AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Algebraic_real_1 , AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Multiplicity_type > in res .
members.argsstring: (AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Polynomial_1 p, AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Bound l, AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Bound u, OutputIterator res)

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Computes all real solutions of $ p$ in the closed interval $ [l,u]$ , and copies them as objects of type AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Algebraic_real_1 in res .
members.detailed: The bool known_to_be_square_free indicates whether $ p$ is known to be square free. Each root, though it might be a multiple root, is reported only once.
members.argsstring: (AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Polynomial_1 p, bool known_to_be_square_free, AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Bound l, AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Bound u, OutputIterator res)

AlgebraicKernel_d_1::SquareFreeFactorize.name: AlgebraicKernel_d_1::SquareFreeFactorize_1
AlgebraicKernel_d_1::SquareFreeFactorize.brief: Computes a square free factorization of an AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Polynomial_1 .
AlgebraicKernel_d_1::SquareFreeFactorize.detailed: A polynomial $ p$ is factored into square free and pairwise coprime non-constant factors $ q_i$ with multiplicities $ m_i$ and a constant factor $ c$ , such that $ p = c \cdot q_1^{m_1} \cdot ... \cdot q_n^{m_n}$ . The factor multiplicity pairs $ <q_i,m_i>$ are written to the given output iterator. The constant factor $ c$ is not computed. Refines Assignable CopyConstructible AlgebraicKernel_d_1::IsSquareFree_1 AlgebraicKernel_d_1::MakeSquareFree_1

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Copies in the output iterator the factors of a square free factorization of $ p$ , with their multiplicity, as objects of type std::pair< AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Polynomial_1 , AlgebraicKernel_d_1::Multiplicity_type > .

members.brief: introduces an AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Algebraic_real_2 initialized to the only real intersection of $ f$ and $ g$ in the open box $ B = (x_l,x_u)\times(y_l,y_u)$ .
members.detailed: $ x_l < x_u$ $ y_l < y_u$ $ f$ is square free. $ g$ is square free. $ f$ and $ g$ are coprime. $ f$ and $ g$ have exactly one common solution in $ B$ $ f$ and $ g$ have no common solution on $ \partial B$
members.argsstring: (AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Polynomial_2 f, AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Polynomial_2 g, AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Bound x_l, AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Bound x_u, AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Bound y_l, AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Bound y_u)

AlgebraicKernel_d_2::IsCoprime_2.name: AlgebraicKernel_d_2::IsCoprime_2
AlgebraicKernel_d_2::IsCoprime_2.brief: Computes whether a given pair of bivariate polynomials is coprime.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Computes whether $ f$ and $ g$ are coprime.

AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Isolate_2.name: AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Isolate_2
AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Isolate_2.brief: Computes an isolating box for a given AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Algebraic_real_2 .
AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Isolate_2.detailed: Refines AdaptableFunctor AlgebraicKernel_d_2::IsolateX_2 AlgebraicKernel_d_2::IsolateY_2 AlgebraicKernel_d_2::ComputePolynomialX_2 AlgebraicKernel_d_2::ComputePolynomialY_2

members.name: operator()
members.brief: The returned std::array $ [xl,xu,yl,yu]$ represents an open isolating box $ B=(xl,xu)\times(yl,yu)$ for $ a$ with respect to $ f$ .
members.detailed: $ f(a)\neq0$ $ a \in B$ . $ \{ r | f(r)=0 \} \cap\overline{B} = \emptyset$ .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: The returned std::array $ [xl,xu,yl,yu]$ represents an open isolating box $ B=(xl,xu)\times(yl,yu)$ for $ a$ with respect to the common solutions of $ f$ and $ g$ .
members.detailed: It is not necessary that $ a$ is a common solution of $ f$ and $ g$ . $ a \in B$ . $ \{ r | f(r)=g(r)=0 \} \cap\overline{B} \in\{\{a\},\emptyset\}$ .

AlgebraicKernel_d_2::IsolateX_2.name: AlgebraicKernel_d_2::IsolateX_2
AlgebraicKernel_d_2::IsolateX_2.brief: Computes an isolating interval for the first coordinate of an AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Algebraic_real_2 with respect to the real roots of a univariate polynomial.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Computes an open isolating interval $ I=(l,u)$ for the first coordinate $ x$ of $ a$ with respect to the real roots of $ p$ .
members.detailed: It is not required that $ x$ is a root of $ p$ . $ x \in I$ . $ p(\alpha) \neq0 | \forall\alpha\in\overline{I}\backslash x$ .

AlgebraicKernel_d_2::IsolateY_2.name: AlgebraicKernel_d_2::IsolateY_2
AlgebraicKernel_d_2::IsolateY_2.brief: Computes an isolating interval for the second coordinate of an AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Algebraic_real_2 with respect to the real roots of a univariate polynomial.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Computes an open isolating interval $ I=(l,u)$ for the second coordinate $ y$ of $ a$ with respect to the real roots of $ p$ .
members.detailed: It is not required that $ x$ is a root of $ p$ . $ y \in I$ . $ p(\alpha) \neq0 | \forall\alpha\in\overline{I}\backslash y$ .

members.brief: Type convertible to bool .

AlgebraicKernel_d_2::MakeCoprime_2.name: AlgebraicKernel_d_2::MakeCoprime_2
AlgebraicKernel_d_2::MakeCoprime_2.brief: Computes for a given pair of bivariate polynomials $ p_1$ , $ p_2$ their common part $ g$ and coprime parts $ q_1$ , $ q_2$ respectively.
AlgebraicKernel_d_2::MakeCoprime_2.detailed: That is, it computes $ g, q_1, q_2$ such that: $ c_1 \cdot p_1 = g \cdot q_1$ for some constant $ c_1$ and $ c_2 \cdot p_2 = g \cdot q_2$ for some constant $ c_2$ , such that $ q_1$ and $ q_2$ are coprime. Refines AdaptableQuinaryFunction AlgebraicKernel_d_2::IsCoprime_2

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Computes $ g, q_1, q_2$ as described above.
members.detailed: Returns whether $ p_1$ and $ p_2$ where already coprime.
members.argsstring: (const AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Polynomial_2 &p1, const AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Polynomial_2 &p2, AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Polynomial_2 &g, AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Polynomial_2 &q1, AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Polynomial_2 &q2)

AlgebraicKernel_d_2::SquareFreeFactorize.name: AlgebraicKernel_d_2::SquareFreeFactorize_2
AlgebraicKernel_d_2::SquareFreeFactorize.brief: Computes a square free factorization of an AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Polynomial_2 .
AlgebraicKernel_d_2::SquareFreeFactorize.detailed: A polynomial $ p$ is factored into square free and pairwise coprime non-constant factors $ q_i$ with multiplicities $ m_i$ and a constant factor $ c$ , such that $ p = c \cdot q_1^{m_1} \cdot ... \cdot q_n^{m_n}$ . The factor multiplicity pairs $ <q_i,m_i>$ are written to the given output iterator. The constant factor $ c$ is not computed. Refines Assignable CopyConstructible AlgebraicKernel_d_2::IsSquareFree_2 AlgebraicKernel_d_2::MakeSquareFree_2

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Copies in the output iterator the factors of a square free factorization of $ p$ , with their multiplicity, as objects of type std::pair< AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Polynomial_2 , AlgebraicKernel_d_2::Multiplicity_type> .

CGAL::Algebraic_kernel_d_1.name: CGAL::Algebraic_kernel_d_1
CGAL::Algebraic_kernel_d_1.brief: The class represents an algebraic real root by a square free polynomial and an isolating interval that uniquely defines the root.
CGAL::Algebraic_kernel_d_1.detailed: The template argument Coeff determines the coefficient type of the kernel, which is also the coefficient type of the supported polynomials. Currently, the following coefficient types are supported: Gmpz , Gmpq , (requires configuration with external libraries GMP, MPFR and MPFI) CORE::BigInt , CORE::BigRat , (requires configuration with external library GMP) leda_integer , leda_rational . (requires configuration with external library LEDA) Advanced The template argument type can also be set to Sqrt_extension<NT,ROOT> , where NT is one of the types listed above. ROOT should be one of the integer types. See also the documentation of Sqrt_extension<NT,ROOT> . The current method to isolate roots is the bitstream Descartes method presented in [6] . The used method to refine the approximation of an algebraic real root is a slightly modified (filtered) version of the one presented in [1] . The method has quadratic convergence. Is model of AlgebraicKernel_d_1 AlgebraicKernel_d_1 Polynomial_d CGAL::Algebraic_kernel_d_2 <Coeff>

CGAL::Algebraic_kernel_d_2.name: CGAL::Algebraic_kernel_d_2
CGAL::Algebraic_kernel_d_2.brief: This class gathers necessary tools for solving and handling bivariate polynomial systems of general degree $ d$ .
CGAL::Algebraic_kernel_d_2.detailed: This class is based on an algorithm computing a geometric-topological analysis of a single curve [5] and of a pair of curves [4] . The main idea behind both analyses is to compute the critical x-coordinates of curves and curve pairs by projection (resultants), and compute additional information about the critical fibers using subresultants and Sturm-Habicht sequences [7] . With that information, the fiber at critical x-coordinates is computed by a variant of the Bitstream Descartes method. See also [8] for a comprehensive description of these techniques. A point $ p$ of type Algebraic_real_2 is represented by its $ x$ -coordinate $ x_0$ (as described in the Algebraic_kernel_d_1 paragraph above), an algebraic curve where $ p$ lies on, and an integer $ i$ , denoting that $ p$ is the $ i$ th point in the fiber at $ x_0$ , counted from the bottom (ignoring a possible vertical line at $ x_0$ ). This determines the point uniquely, but the $ y$ -coordinate is not stored internally in terms of an Algebraic_real_1 object. Querying such a representation by calling Compute_y_2 is a time-consuming step, and should be avoided for efficiency reasons if possible. Note that this representation is not exposed in the interface. The template argument Coeff determines the coefficient type of the kernel, which is also the innermost coefficient type of the supported polynomials. Currently, the following coefficient types are supported: Gmpz , Gmpq , (requires configuration with external libraries GMP, MPFR and MPFI) CORE::BigInt , CORE::BigRat , (requires configuration with external library GMP) leda_integer , leda_rational . (requires configuration with external library LEDA) Advanced The template argument type can also be set to Sqrt_extension<NT,ROOT> , where NT is one of the types listed above. ROOT should be one of the integer types. See also the documentation of Sqrt_extension<NT,ROOT> . Is model of AlgebraicKernel_d_2 AlgebraicKernel_d_1 AlgebraicKernel_d_2 Polynomial_d CGAL::Algebraic_kernel_d_2 <Coeff>

PkgAlgebraicKernelDRef.name: PkgAlgebraicKernelDRef
PkgAlgebraicKernelDRef.detailed: Eric Berberich, Michael Hemmer, Michael Kerber, Sylvain Lazard, Luis Peñaranda, and Monique Teillaud Real solving of polynomials is a fundamental problem with a wide application range. This package is targeted to provide black-box implementation algorithms to determine, compare and approximate real roots of univariate polynomials and bivariate polynomial systems. Such a black-box is called an Algebraic Kernel . So far the package only provides models for the univariate kernel. Nevertheless, it already defines concepts for the bivariate kernel, since this settles the interface for upcoming implementations.
